steps.title: Interpret function notation
steps.content: In the function $K(v)$, the input $v$ represents speed in m/s and the output $K(v)$ represents kinetic energy in joules. The notation $K(34) = 5,202$ means: when we substitute $v = 34$, the function outputs \$5,202$. Therefore, the object traveling at \$34$ m/s has kinetic energy of \$5,202$ joules. That matches choice A — A is correct.
